flashcards.back: A communication method where data is sent without establishing a dedicated connection first. Each packet (datagram) is independent and contains all addressing information needed for delivery.
A communication method that requires establishing a logical connection (virtual circuit) before data transfer. It involves connection setup, data transfer, and connection teardown phases.

flashcards.front: Name the protocol that uses connectionless service.
flashcards.back: UDP (User Datagram Protocol) - it is the primary connectionless transport layer protocol in TCP/IP suite.

flashcards.front: Name the protocol that uses connection-oriented service.
flashcards.back: TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) - it provides reliable, connection-oriented communication at the transport layer.
A self-contained packet that carries all necessary information (source/destination addresses) to be routed independently without relying on previous or future packets.
A logical connection established between two devices before data transfer. It creates a path through the network that all packets will follow, identified by a connection ID.

flashcards.front: What are the three phases of connection-oriented communication?
flashcards.back: 1) Connection Establishment (three-way handshake), 2) Data Transfer, 3) Connection Termination (four-way handshake).

flashcards.front: What is the three-way handshake in TCP?
flashcards.back: A process using SYN, SYN-ACK, and ACK packets to establish a connection: 1) Client sends SYN, 2) Server responds with SYN-ACK, 3) Client sends ACK.

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of connectionless communication?
flashcards.back: Lower overhead and faster transmission since no connection setup is required. It has less latency and is suitable for real-time applications.

flashcards.front: What is the main disadvantage of connection-oriented communication?
flashcards.back: Higher overhead due to connection setup and maintenance. It introduces latency and consumes more network resources.
Connection-oriented service (TCP) provides reliable delivery with error checking, sequencing, and flow control.

flashcards.front: Which service has larger header size?
flashcards.back: Connection-oriented (TCP) has larger headers (20-60 bytes) vs connectionless (UDP) headers (8 bytes) due to additional control information.
No, connectionless service is stateless - each packet is independent with no memory of previous transmissions.
Yes, it maintains state information about the connection including sequence numbers, acknowledgment numbers, and window size.

flashcards.front: Which layer in OSI model handles connection-oriented services?
flashcards.back: The Transport Layer (Layer 4) handles both connection-oriented (TCP) and connectionless (UDP) services.
